metadata.title: Abiraterone and prednisolone Therapy (mCRPC)
metadata.indication: Abiraterone with prednisone/prednisolone for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer after docetaxel or when chemotherapy is not yet clinically indicated.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Any independently actionable entered value is assessed; omitted domains remain explicitly unassessed and never block a partial result.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by the appropriately qualified Consultant specified in the NCCP source.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification or non-standard continuation should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the official NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

bilirubin_uln_multiple.label: Bilirubin result (actual value entered in UI; ×ULN calculated automatically)

alt_ast_uln_multiple.label: ALT / AST result (actual value entered in UI; highest ×ULN calculated automatically)

creatinine_below_1_5_uln.label: Creatinine is <1.5 × the reporting laboratory ULN
creatinine_below_1_5_uln.help_text: The male creatinine ULN is laboratory-specific; use the report flag/reference range.

toxicity_grade.label: Worst current abiraterone-related non-haematological toxicity

options.label: Grade 0 — No toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of this adverse event.

options.label: Grade 1 — Mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — Moderate
options.description: Moderate; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — Severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation may be indicated; limits self-care activities of daily living.

toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the exact adverse event, assess symptoms and functional impact, and apply the toxicity-specific CTCAE v5.0 definition. Do not grade solely from a generic severity impression.

significant_cardiac_disease.label: Clinically significant cardiac disease / baseline LVEF <50%

active_hepatitis_or_chronic_liver_diseas.label: Active/symptomatic viral hepatitis or relevant chronic liver disease

output_templates.proceed: No encoded restriction was triggered in the entered domain(s). This is not an overall treatment clearance.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; all omitted clinically relevant domains remain unassessed.
output_templates.consultant_review: The entered value requires protocol-specific clinical review.
output_templates.withhold: Withhold and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.
output_templates.delay: Delay treatment and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.

action.message: ECOG is outside the NCCP eligibility range 0–2.

rules.explanation: ECOG is outside the NCCP eligibility range 0–2.

action.message: Bilirubin does not meet the encoded NCCP eligibility criterion <1.5 ×ULN.

rules.explanation: Bilirubin does not meet the encoded NCCP eligibility criterion <1.5 ×ULN.

action.message: ALT/AST do not meet the encoded NCCP eligibility criterion <2.5 ×ULN.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST do not meet the encoded NCCP eligibility criterion <2.5 ×ULN.

action.message: ALP is ≥6 × the configured CUH ULN (120 U/L); NCCP eligibility requires <6 ×ULN.

rules.explanation: ALP is ≥6 × the configured CUH ULN (120 U/L); NCCP eligibility requires <6 ×ULN.

action.message: Creatinine does not meet the NCCP eligibility criterion <1.5 × local ULN.

rules.explanation: Creatinine does not meet the NCCP eligibility criterion <1.5 × local ULN.

action.message: Correct hypokalaemia before treatment; NCCP requires potassium >3.5 mmol/L and recommends maintaining >4.0 where possible.

rules.explanation: Correct hypokalaemia before treatment; NCCP requires potassium >3.5 mmol/L and recommends maintaining >4.0 where possible.

action.message: Use 25% of the original abiraterone dose for Child-Pugh B according to the NCCP source.

rules.explanation: Use 25% of the original abiraterone dose for Child-Pugh B according to the NCCP source.

action.message: Avoid abiraterone in Child-Pugh C severe hepatic impairment.

rules.explanation: Avoid abiraterone in Child-Pugh C severe hepatic impairment.

action.message: Withhold for Grade ≥3 toxicity and treat medically; do not restart until Grade 1 or baseline.

rules.explanation: Withhold for Grade ≥3 toxicity and treat medically; do not restart until Grade 1 or baseline.

action.message: Abiraterone with predniSONE/prednisoLONE is contraindicated in combination with radium-223.

rules.explanation: Abiraterone with predniSONE/prednisoLONE is contraindicated in combination with radium-223.

action.message: Clinically significant cardiac disease requires consultant review before treatment.

rules.explanation: Clinically significant cardiac disease requires consultant review before treatment.

action.message: The entered hepatic history meets an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: The entered hepatic history meets an NCCP exclusion.

action.message: History of adrenal dysfunction is an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: History of adrenal dysfunction is an NCCP exclusion.

action.message: Visceral metastases are listed as an exclusion in the NCCP mCRPC regimen.

rules.explanation: Visceral metastases are listed as an exclusion in the NCCP mCRPC regimen.

indications.description: Abiraterone with prednisone/prednisolone for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer after docetaxel or when chemotherapy is not yet clinically indicated.

administration.instructions: Take without food, at least 1 hour before or 2 hours after eating; swallow whole.

administration.dose: 10 mg daily or 5 mg twice daily

treatment.additional_requirement: Maintain medical androgen-deprivation therapy unless surgically castrated.

regular: FBC, renal/liver profile, glucose and blood pressure every 2 weeks for cycles 1–3, then every 4 weeks

supportive_care.mapping_basis: The NCCP regimen classifies this oral SACT as minimal emetogenic risk; the linked oral minimal-to-low supportive-care guidance is used rather than a parenteral prescription sheet.
supportive_care.other: Prednisolone may require gradual withdrawal when abiraterone is stopped.
